Instructions

Section I of this exam contains 60 multiple-choice questions. Fill in only the circles fornumbers 1 through 60 on your answer sheet.

Indicate all of your answers to the multiple-choice questions on the answer sheet. Nocredit will be given for anything written in this exam booklet, but you may use the bookletfor notes or scratch work. After you have decided which of the suggested answers is best,
completely fill in the corresponding circle on the answer sheet. Give only one answer toeach question. If you change an answer, be sure that the previous mark is erasedcompletely. Here is a sample question and answer.

Sample Question Sample AnswerChicago is a @®@eOQO®

options.A: state
options.B: city
options.C: country
options.D: continent
options.E:
village

Use your time effectively, working as quickly as you can without losing accuracy. Do notspend too much time on any one question. Go on to other questions and come back tothe ones you have not answered if you have time. It is not expected that everyone willknow the answers to all of the multiple-choice questions.

Your total score on the multiple-choice section is based only on the number of questionsanswered correctly. Points are not deducted for incorrect answers or unansweredquestions.

---PAGE 4---
The inclusion of source material in this exam is not intended as anendorsement by the College Board or ETS of the content, ideas, orvalues expressed in the material. The material has been selected bythe economics faculty who serve on the AP MacroeconomicsDevelopment Committee. In their judgment, the material printed herereflects various aspects of the course of study on which this exam isbased and is therefore appropriate to use to measure the skills andknowledge of this course.

---PAGE 5---

Time—1 hour and 10 minutes
60 Questions

Directions: Each of the questions or incomplete statements below is followed by five suggested answers orcompletions. Select the one that is best in each case and then fill in the corresponding circle on the answer sheet.

text:
Which of the following typically occurs during an 5. Which of the following will most likely result

expansionary phase of a business cycle?

options.A: Increased nominal interest rates
options.B: Increased business profits
options.C: Increased real value of fixed incomes
options.D: Decreased purchasing power of cash
options.E: Decreased real wealth

text:
Which of the following is a defining characteristic

of a fractional reserve banking system?

options.A: The existence of a central bank witha monopoly on money creation
options.B: The use of paper money backed bya commodity such as gold or silver
options.C: The fact that banks retain an amount of bankreserves that is less than the amount ofcustomer demand deposits
options.D: The requirement that banks maintain a certainpercentage of their reserves as a deposit inan account at the central bank
options.E: The regulations that separate investmentbanking from commercial banking

text:
A negative aggregate supply shock will result in

which of the following in the short run?

options.A: An increase in the price level and a decreasein the unemployment rate
options.B: A decrease in the price level and an increasein the unemployment rate
options.C: A decrease in both the price level and realoutput
options.D: An increase in both the price level and realoutput
options.E:
An increase in both the price level and theunemployment rate

text:
10.

Which of the following combinations of fiscal

and monetary policies will correct a severe

recession?

options.A: Aggregate demand will decrease as a result ofa decrease in the price level.
options.B: Aggregate demand will increase as a result ofan increase in investment spending.
options.C: Aggregate demand will increase as a result ofan increase in exports.
options.D: Aggregate demand will not change, sinceconsumer spending has not changed.
options.E: Aggregate demand will not change, sinceinvestment spending has not changed.

Suppose that a country’s nominal gross domesticproduct (GDP) was $1,000 in year 1 and $2,000

in year 2. If year 1 is the base year and real GDPin year 2 was $1,000, which of the following

is true?

options.A: The United States dollar will appreciate inforeign exchange markets.
options.B: Household savings in the United States willdecrease.
options.C: The United States exports will increase.
options.D: The demand for United States dollars willdecrease.
options.E:
Private investment in plant and equipment inthe United States will increase.

text: An economy experiences a sharp increasein energy prices, and policy makers adopta stabilization policy to increase aggregatedemand. Compared with the initial short-runequilibrium, which of the following willdefinitely occur?

options.C: Lower price level
options.D: Higher price level
options.E: Higher aggregate supply

options.C: increase by $150
options.D: increase by $750

text: Which of the following is true when interestrates rise?

The graph above shows the productionpossibilities curves (PPC) for an economy.
The concept of opportunity cost is bestrepresented by which of the following?

text: If the central bank of Country K wishes toincrease the value of its currency on foreignexchange markets, it can do which of thefollowing?

options.A: Buy the currencies of other countries
options.B: Increase the domestic money supply inCountry K
options.C: Increase the income tax in Country K
options.D: Raise interest rates in Country K
options.E: Increase tariffs in Country K

options.C: long-run Phillips curve to shift to the left

options.E: actual inflation rate to fall below the expectedinflation rate

When the total amount the government spendsequals tax revenues in any given year, which ofthe following must remain constant?

options.A: Nominal wages fall, shifting the short-runaggregate supply curve to the right.
options.B: Nominal wages fall, shifting the short-runaggregate supply curve to the left.

options.D: Nominal wages rise, shifting the short-runaggregate supply curve to the right.

text:
Which of the following statements about inflation

is true?

options.A: The expected inflation rate is the differencebetween nominal and real interest rates.
options.B: Low expected inflation rates lead to highinflation rates.
options.C: Lenders lose from expected inflation.
options.D: Lenders gain from unexpected inflation.
options.E: Workers lose from expected inflation.

text: The opportunity cost of an activity is

options.B: the value of the benefit received fromperforming the activity
options.C: the value of the forgone benefit of the nextbest alternative
options.D: zero if the activity offered no benefits
options.E:
the sum of benefits from all the sacrificedalternatives

Instructions

The questions for Section II are printed in this orange booklet. You may use the pages inthis booklet to organize your answers and for scratch work, but you must write youranswers in the separate Section II: Free Response booklet. No credit will be given for anywork written in this orange booklet.

The proctor will announce the beginning and end of the reading period. You are advisedto spend the 10-minute period reading all the questions and planning your answers. Youmay begin writing your responses before the reading period is over.

Write clearly and legibly. Begin your response to each question at the top of a new page;
completely fill in the circle at the top of each page that corresponds to the question youare answering. Cross out any errors you make; crossed-out work will not be scored.

Manage your time carefully. You may proceed freely from one question to the next. Youmay review your responses if you finish before the end of the exam is announced.

ITotal Time—1 hourReading Period—10 minutesWriting Period —50 minutes

Directions: You are advised to spend the first 10 minutes reading all of the questions and planning youranswers. You will then have 50 minutes to answer all three of the following questions. You may beginwriting your responses before the reading period is over. It is suggested that you spend approximately halfyour time on the first question and divide the remaining time equally between the next two questions.
Include correctly labeled diagrams, if useful or required, in explaining your answers. A correctly labeleddiagram must have all axes and curves clearly labeled and must show directional changes. If the question

prompts you to "Calculate," you must show how you arrived at your final answer. Use a pen with black ordark blue ink.

Assume a country’s economy is currently in recession.

(a) Draw a correctly labeled graph of the long-run aggregate supply, short-run aggregate supply, and aggregatedemand curves, and show each of the following.

(i) Current real output, labeled Y,, and current price level, labeled PL,

(ii) Full employment output, labeled Y;

(b) Assuming the banking system has ample reserves, identify one action the central bank can take to help theeconomy recover from the recession.

options.C:
Draw a correctly labeled graph of the reserve market, and show the impact of the central bank’s actionidentified in part (b) on the policy rate.

(d) On your graph for part (a), show the effect of the central bank’s action identified in part (b) on real outputand the price level.

(e) Assume there is an increase in business confidence as a result of the central bank’s action.
(i) What will happen to the demand for capital goods?

(ii) Draw a correctly labeled graph of the loanable funds market, and show the effect of the changeidentified in part (e)(1) on the real interest rate.

(f) Given your answer to part (e), what is the effect on potential real output in the long run? Explain.
